name: Between Vineyards and Cherry Trees Trail
name_original: 'Entre Viñas y Cerezos' – circular desde Corullón
description: Hike the moderate "Entre Viñas y Cerezos" circular trail from Corullón, winding through vineyards and cherry orchards for 4.9 miles.
region_intro: Hiking around Corullón is characterized by a hilly landscape with ancient, slate-covered slopes that rise to mountain elevations between 500 and 1000 meters. The terrain features steep sections and old vineyards, providing varied hiking opportunities. Local soils are a mix of metal-minerals, layered slate, quartzite, sandstone, and clay, contributing to the region's distinct natural environment. This rugged topography offers diverse trails for outdoor exploration. Best hiking trails around Corullón The most popular hiking route is Between Vineyards and Cherry Trees Trail, a 4.9 miles (7.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cacabelos River Beach – Viña Los Almendros Cabin loop from Castro de la Ventosa, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This trail features riverside scenery and passes by a cabin. Local hikers also love the Pereje village – View of Villafranca del Bierzo loop from Pereje, a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail leading through village surroundings and offering views of Villafranca del Bierzo, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Corullón is defined by hilly landscapes, slate-covered slopes, and old vineyards. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Corullón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 2500 hikers have used komoot to explore Corullón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general terrain like for hiking around Corullón?
A: Hiking around Corullón is characterized by a hilly landscape with ancient, slate-covered slopes that rise to mountain elevations between 500 and 1000 meters. The terrain features steep sections and old vineyards, offering a rugged yet scenic environment for exploration. The local soils are a unique mix of metal-minerals, layered slate, quartzite, sandstone, and clay.

Q: Are there hiking routes suitable for families in Corullón?
A: Yes, Corullón offers several routes suitable for families. While the region is known for its rugged topography, there are 11 easy trails available. These routes provide a great way to enjoy the landscape without overly challenging terrain, though specific family-friendly features like playgrounds or picnic areas would need to be checked per route.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes in Corullón?
A: Corullón's hikes often feature impressive natural and historical landmarks. You can explore ancient Roman mining systems like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3444048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orellán Galleries&lt;/a&gt;, which are underground water conduits culminating in a viewpoint with stunning vistas. The region also boasts diverse flora and 'damp mountain soil aromas' due to its Atlantic character. Nearby, you can find several lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5550699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lago de Carucedo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553433" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarrubia Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Corullón area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Corullón are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38148915" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cacabelos River Beach – Viña Los Almendros Cabin loop from Castro de la Ventosa&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.0-mile (12.9 km) path featuring riverside scenery. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149711" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pereje village – View of Villafranca del Bierzo loop from Pereje&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 9.8-mile (15.8 km) trail offering views of Villafranca del Bierzo.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Corullón?
A: The region's climate is influenced by its 'Atlantic character,' suggesting a diverse flora and 'damp mountain soil aromas.' While specific best seasons aren't detailed, the varied landscape with vineyards and cherry trees implies pleasant conditions during spring and autumn. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring cooler, damper weather, but hiking is generally possible year-round depending on personal preference and trail conditions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Corullón?
A: The trails in Corullón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from ancient slate-covered slopes to old vineyards, and the diverse options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The scenic views and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites are frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical sites or cultural attractions accessible from the hiking trails?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/654798" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villafranca del Bierzo Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4838119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santiago (Holy Door of Forgiveness)&lt;/a&gt; in nearby Villafranca del Bierzo, which is a significant stop on the Camino de Santiago. These historical landmarks can be integrated into longer hiking routes, offering cultural enrichment alongside natural exploration.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of hiking trails in Corullón?
A: Corullón offers a range of difficulty levels to suit different hikers. Out of over 40 trails, there are 11 easy routes, 22 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes. This variety ensures that both casual walkers and experienced hikers can find suitable challenges.

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads in Corullón?
A: While specific parking details for individual trailheads are not provided, Corullón is a rural area, and parking is generally available in or near villages and popular starting points for trails. It is advisable to check local signage or specific route descriptions for precise parking information before your hike.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging long-distance hikes in the area?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Corullón offers several difficult routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38149749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Duro of Pereje – View of Villafranca del Bierzo loop from Pereje&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 17.6-mile (28.4 km) trail with significant elevation changes. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40005234" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viña Los Almendros Cabin – View of Villafranca del Bierzo loop from Cerro de Villagote&lt;/a&gt;, covering 16.9 miles (27.3 km) with over 770 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Corullón?
A: Generally, most hiking trails in natural areas like Corullón are dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash and owners practice responsible pet etiquette, such as cleaning up after them. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage, particularly in protected areas or private lands, to ensure dogs are permitted.

Q: Are there any cafes or pubs in Corullón village or near the hiking areas?
A: Corullón is a village, and while specific establishments aren't listed, it's common for small villages in Spain to have local cafes, bars, or restaurants where hikers can find refreshments and meals. These can be good places to start or end a hike, offering a taste of local hospitality and cuisine.
tips: Corullón Castle is located in the El Bierzo region. It was built as a mansion and recreation by the Rodríguez de Valcarce family in the 14th century. The castle stands on a Roman fort and a medieval tower. Throughout its history, it belonged to various families, including the Álvarez Osorio and the Álvarez de Toledo. Currently, it is owned by the descendants of the Marquises of Villafranca. Although it cannot be visited, its history and architecture make it a historical and artistic monument of the region. 🏰🌟

Corullón, in the El Bierzo region, is a place where nature unfolds at its maximum expression. Its chestnut groves, with numerous centuries-old specimens, hide a world of sensations. Along endless paths, you can discover interesting flora and fauna. In addition, Corullón is characterized by its vineyards, cherry trees and orchard products. If you want to explore more, I recommend the Route ‘Between Vineyards and Cherry Trees’, famous for the spectacular flowering of cherry trees at the end of March and beginning of April. In August or early September, the vineyards take center stage, combining reddish, green and ocher colors. An unforgettable experience in the middle of Bercian nature! 🌳🍇🌸

The Mirador de Corullón invites you to immerse yourself in centuries of history and discover the essence of Bierzo. Located at LE-5220, 24514 Corullón, this beautiful enclave offers you exuberant nature so you can enjoy the fresh air of the mountains. From the viewpoint, you can contemplate the impressive views of the mountains, rivers, forests, cherry groves and vineyards that characterize the region. In addition, the Corullón City Council has installed 25 tables and benches so you can enjoy the panoramic view and have a picnic in this place. If you visit Corullón, don't miss this unique and unforgettable experience. 🌄🍷🌸

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the circular hike?
faqs.answer: The 'Entre Viñas y Cerezos' circular route is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 33 minutes to complete. This duration is based on an average hiking pace and can vary depending on your speed and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: While komoot classifies this route as moderate, it is often described as 'simple' and 'very easy to walk' by locals, making it suitable for families. The path winds through vineyards and orchards, offering a pleasant experience for those new to hiking or looking for a less strenuous outing.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: This trail offers a beautiful blend of natural and historical sights. You'll hike through extensive vineyards and numerous cherry orchards, especially stunning during spring bloom. Key landmarks include the historic Castillo de Corullón, a scenic Castaño Forest Trail Near Corullón, and the Corullón Viewpoint, which provides impressive panoramic vistas.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: The absolute best time to hike this trail is during late March and early April. This is when the cherry trees burst into bloom, transforming the landscape into a spectacular 'mantle of pink and white flowers.' This period is often referred to as 'Bierzo's Sakura' and offers a truly unique experience.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead in Corullón?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead isn't detailed, but Corullón is a small town. You should be able to find parking within the village, likely near the town center or close to the starting point of the circular route. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the 'Entre Viñas y Cerezos' trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Entre Viñas y Cerezos' trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, as it passes through agricultural areas like vineyards and orchards, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash to respect private property and local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the 'Entre Viñas y Cerezos' circular route. It's a publicly accessible trail through the natural landscape of Corullón.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain primarily consists of paths winding through vineyards, ancient chestnut groves, and cherry orchards. You'll encounter some sections on schist soils. While there's an elevation gain of about 337 meters, the paths are generally described as 'simple' and 'very easy to walk,' making for a pleasant hiking experience.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options near the trailhead in Corullón?
faqs.answer: Corullón is a small, charming village. While specific establishments aren't listed, you can typically find local cafes and restaurants offering traditional cuisine. For accommodation, there might be guesthouses or rural lodgings available. It's recommended to check local listings or inquire in the village upon arrival.

faqs.question: What makes the 'Entre Viñas y Cerezos' trail particularly special?
faqs.answer: This trail is unique for its spectacular cherry blossom display in spring, earning Corullón the nickname 'Bierzo's Sakura.' It also offers an immersive experience into the local wine culture, winding directly 'between vineyards' renowned for high-quality Mencía wines. It's a perfect blend of natural beauty and agricultural heritage.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead in Corullón?
faqs.answer: Information on direct public transport to Corullón for hiking access is limited. It's generally recommended to travel by car to reach the village and the trailhead. If relying on public transport, you might need to take a bus to a larger nearby town in El Bierzo and then arrange for local transport to Corullón.
